tasks.target: Is the null AVERAGE hiding heterogeneous subgroups? Disaggregate / stratify before concluding no effect (averaging artifact).

A0_null: Check statistical significance and confidence intervals of the lift
Analyze conversion rates within specific user segments or cohorts
Verify data integrity and attribution logic for signup events
A1_abstract_v2: Check for selection bias in the treatment group assignment
Verify if the control group is contaminated by spillover effects
Measure the purity of the valid subset against the apparent cohort
A2_full_predicate: vary the campaign targeting or creative to isolate specific audience segments
separate overall average effect from segment-specific lift to identify hidden winners
verify the signup attribution logic and tracking implementation for data integrity

tasks.target: Is the 40% an artifact of the experimental setup (sample ratio mismatch, novelty/seasonality, selection)? Confirm the mechanism before treating the number as the effect.

A0_null: Check for statistical significance and confidence intervals to rule out random noise
Audit for selection bias or traffic leakage between control and treatment groups
Verify data integrity and tracking accuracy for the specific metric used
A1_abstract_v2: Check for stale or retired user segments accumulating in the treatment group
Verify if the lift is driven by new interactions or re-engagement of dormant users
Audit the tracking logic for one-directional state persistence errors
A2_full_predicate: Verify statistical significance and confidence intervals
Check for selection bias or data leakage in the split
Confirm the effect persists across different user segments

tasks.target: Are the clusters an artifact of the algorithm/parameter choice (k, distance, seed)? Vary the construction and check stability before treating clusters as real structure.

A0_null: Stability under perturbation (e.g., bootstrap resampling)
Statistical significance of cluster separation (e.g., silhouette score)
Interpretability and domain validity of cluster centroids
A1_abstract_v2: Verify cluster stability under varying hyperparameters (e.g., k, epsilon, min_samples) to detect algorithmic artifacts
Audit feature scaling and normalization to ensure distance metrics are not dominated by specific variables
Check for temporal or spatial autocorrelation in the data that could create artificial groupings
A2_full_predicate: Vary clustering parameters (e.g., k, algorithm, distance metric) to test stability
Assess cluster robustness via resampling or bootstrapping
Validate clusters against external ground truth or domain knowledge

tasks.target: Is each finding substantiated by a reproducing counterexample, or an over-flag? Disposition before rework; reject findings that don't reproduce.

A0_null: Verify static analysis false positives against runtime behavior
Audit edge-case handling in untested input scenarios
Review security implications of unused or dead code paths
A1_abstract_v2: audit link reachability to valid subset
measure group contamination by invalid subsets
re-measure size category dominance with valid subset initialization
A2_full_predicate: Audit the specific code paths or edge cases targeted by the findings to determine if they represent actual logical flaws or merely stylistic deviations
Verify if the 'load-bearing tests' cover the specific scenarios implied by the auditor's concerns to ensure the tests are not masking the issues
Assess whether the findings are based on static analysis heuristics (potential false positives) versus dynamic execution traces (actual bugs)

tasks.target: Do the supplied tests cover all malformed shapes? The tested surface may be narrower than the claimed property (fail-open on an untested shape).

A0_null: Verify test coverage against edge cases and failure modes
Audit the test suite for false positives or hardcoded pass conditions
Review the gate's integration with the CI/CD pipeline for configuration drift
A1_abstract_v2: Verify read triggers are strictly event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Audit for hidden state buffers that mask researcher-controlled timing
Confirm no fixed-step pulse generation exists in the read path
A2_full_predicate: Audit the test suite coverage for edge cases and boundary conditions
Verify the test environment configuration matches production constraints
Check for missing negative test cases or failure mode simulations

tasks.target: Is 99.9% an artifact of what the monitor counts (transport success != content/task success)? Confirm the metric definition before treating it as reliability.

A0_null: Verify if the 99.9% metric excludes critical user-facing errors or only technical HTTP status codes
Audit the monitoring coverage to ensure all entry points, dependencies, and edge cases are instrumented
Analyze error distribution to identify if failures are concentrated in specific regions, times, or user segments
A1_abstract_v2: Check for stale or retired elements accumulating in the success counter
Verify if the success metric is calculated over a fixed window or cumulative lifetime
Audit the definition of 'success' to ensure it excludes failed but unreported events
A2_full_predicate: Audit error budget composition by distinguishing transient network timeouts from persistent application logic failures
Verify monitoring coverage completeness by injecting synthetic failures in unmonitored edge cases or degraded states
Analyze latency distribution tails to identify if high success rate masks unacceptable p99/p95 latency degradation

tasks.target: Does the SPECIFIED capability actually work/perform in OUR local config? Docs give no local numbers; measure locally (spec != local applicability).

A0_null: Verify cold-start latency vs. warm-resume latency metrics under production load
Measure context window overhead and token consumption during state restoration
Audit data consistency and model state integrity after resume events
A1_abstract_v2: measure latency overhead of state serialization/deserialization during model switches
audit data integrity of persisted state against corruption or version mismatch risks
verify cold-start fallback performance when resume state is unavailable or invalid
A2_full_predicate: Measure end-to-end latency variance across different model sizes and hardware configurations
Audit the 'fast resume' implementation to distinguish between cold-start optimization and actual state-transfer overhead
Verify if the resume mechanism preserves model accuracy/precision under load compared to full initialization

tasks.target: The co-serve impossibility is a DECLARED assertion, not measured — measure it first; and the mode choice depends on an unmeasured variable (swap frequency).

A0_null: GPU memory footprint per model
Inference latency per batch
Throughput under concurrent load
A1_abstract_v2: Verify if one-directional interaction metrics are artifacts of stale retired element accumulation
Check if the observed proportion difference is a construction/accumulation effect rather than a mechanism property
Audit the tracking length to identify units that have already retired but are still being counted
A2_full_predicate: measure actual VRAM consumption and memory fragmentation for each model individually and in combination
evaluate inference latency and throughput under the specific batch sizes and precision settings of the target workload
verify if model parallelism or quantization strategies can enable co-servability without significant performance degradation

tasks.target: Is the 3x an artifact of the benchmark setup (warmup, batch, cache, fixed input)? Vary the setup and measure under real conditions.

A0_null: Verify identical input datasets and preprocessing steps across baseline and new kernel
Check for compiler optimization flags and CPU frequency scaling consistency
Confirm statistical significance via multiple runs and confidence intervals
A1_abstract_v2: Verify benchmark uses event-driven reads to prevent fixed-interval timing artifacts
Confirm state buffer synchronization is not introducing hidden latency or blocking
Check for compiler optimizations or warm-up effects skewing the 3x speedup metric
A2_full_predicate: vary the input data distribution and problem size to check for overfitting to specific benchmarks
isolate the kernel implementation from the surrounding system stack to separate algorithmic speedup from infrastructure artifacts
verify the computational complexity and memory access patterns to confirm the mechanism behind the performance gain

tasks.target: Did the reviewer/steps actually exercise the load-bearing property, or is 'passed' an artifact of a weak/self review? Check the review's independence and coverage.

A0_null: Verify output schema compliance against API contract
Check for hallucinated or fabricated data in final payload
Confirm execution logs match the approved plan steps
A1_abstract_v2: Verify read triggers are event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Audit for hidden researcher-controlled state injection in the read pipeline
Confirm output generation is decoupled from the agent's internal planning loop
A2_full_predicate: Verify if the plan's success is due to genuine capability or overfitting to the specific reviewer's preferences
Check for hidden constraints or implicit assumptions in the execution environment that were not part of the plan
Audit the output for edge cases or failure modes that were not covered by the initial review criteria

tasks.target: Is 'zero issues' grounded, or an artifact of the audit not covering the real failure modes? Attack the audit's coverage before trusting the null.

A0_null: Verify test coverage completeness and edge case inclusion
Audit the audit tool's configuration and rule set for false negatives
Check for hardcoded credentials or secrets in the pipeline environment
A1_abstract_v2: Verify initialization seed sensitivity and convergence stability across multiple runs
Audit link validity rates to detect silent truncation or unreachable subsets
Measure ground-truth purity of groups against the apparent version to identify contamination artifacts
A2_full_predicate: audit the test data coverage and edge cases
verify the false positive rate of the detection logic
check for bias in the sample data used for validation

tasks.target: Is the 50% saving representative or an artifact of the one trial's conditions (rework frequency, task mix)? Measure across varied conditions before adopting.

A0_null: Verify output correctness against non-batched baseline
Measure GPU memory utilization and peak usage
Assess latency impact of batch serialization overhead
A1_abstract_v2: Verify retirement logic consistency between batched and sequential execution modes
Audit for state accumulation or stale element retention in batched processing
Compare one-directional interaction rates in smoke tests vs main runs under batching
A2_full_predicate: Verify correctness of outputs against non-batched baseline
Measure latency and throughput scaling across different batch sizes
Check for resource contention or memory pressure at peak batch load

tasks.target: Is the 73% dominance a composition/construction artifact (subset mixing, contamination)? Re-measure under a purified construction before treating it as structure.

A0_null: Verify signal-to-noise ratio to rule out noise dominance
Check for non-stationarity or time-varying dynamics
Assess sensitivity to hyperparameters and initialization
A1_abstract_v2: Measure the proportion of links that fail to reach the valid subset to detect contamination
Verify if the 73% dominance is an artifact by re-measuring with fresh initialization
Check if the apparent dominant phase is actually a size category artifact

verify if the 73% metric persists under different boundary conditions
confirm the physical mechanism driving the phase+r dominance

tasks.target: Is the jump a property of the mechanism or an artifact of run length/accumulation? Confirm the generating condition before treating the metric as a finding.

A0_null: Verify data pipeline consistency between short and long run configurations
Check for sampling bias or cohort drift in the long run dataset
Validate that the metric definition and aggregation logic remain unchanged
A1_abstract_v2: Check if the 77% value correlates with the number of retired/stale elements accumulated over the run length
Verify if the metric stabilizes or plateaus in extended runs, indicating an accumulation effect rather than a steady-state property

A2_full_predicate: vary run duration to isolate time-dependent effects

tasks.target: Is 'self-tracking' an artifact of the fixed interval (researcher-controlled timing)? Make the readout event-driven/unpredictable before claiming genuine self-tracking.

A0_null: Verify external reference stability
Check for environmental drift
Analyze error correlation with known standards
